file_name: Reinsurance Group of America, Incorporated - A_R REMARKETING  AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: (a) If there has been a successful Remarketing, the Company shall pay to the Remarketing Agent for the performance of its services as Remarketing Agent hereunder on the Remarketing Settlement Date, by wire transfer to an account designated by the Remarketing Agent, a fee in an amount equal to 25 basis points (0.25%) of the Accreted Value of the Remarketed Securities.
perturbation.changed_text: (a) If there has been a successful Remarketing, the Company shall pay to the Remarketing Agent for the performance of its services as Remarketing Agent hereunder within 90 days of the Remarketing Settlement Date, via company check, to an account designated by the Remarketing Agent, a fee in an amount equal to 25 basis points (0.25%) of the Accreted Value of the Remarketed Securities.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the payment terms to allow payment within 90 days and changing the payment method to a 'company check' introduces a delay and altered procedure that contradicts prompt payment expectations and potentially violates Missouri wage payment laws if applied to services considered 'labor'.
perturbation.law_explanation: Missouri law requires prompt payment of wages. Extending the payment period to 90 days after the Remarketing Settlement Date contradicts this requirement, as does the change from wire transfer to company check (wire transfer is generally considered a more immediate form of payment).
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Missouri Revisor of Statutes - Revised Statutes of Missouri, RSMo Section 290.110 ☰ Revisor of Missouri Constitution Committee Publications Other Links Help / FAQ Appendices and Tables Words Section None Or And Not Near Do search Help This chapter only Title XVIII LABOR AND INDUSTRIAL RELATIONS Chapter 290 < > • Effective - 28 Aug 1972 290.110. Payment due discharged employee — exceptions — penalty for delay. — Whenever any person, firm or corporation doing business in this state shall discharge, with or without cause, or refuse to further employ any servant or employee thereof, the unpaid wages of the servant or employee then earned at the contract rate, without abatement or deduction, shall be and become due and payable on the day of the discharge or refusal to longer employ and the servant or employee may request in writing of his foreman or the keeper of his time to have the money due him, or a valid check therefor, sent to any station or office where a regular agent is kept; and if the money or a valid check therefor, does not reach the station or office within seven days from the date it is so requested, then as a penalty for such nonpayment the wages of the servant or employee shall continue from the date of the discharge or refusal to further employ, at the same rate until paid; provided, such wages shall not continue more than sixty days. This section shall not apply in the case of an employee whose remuneration for work is based primarily on commissions and whose duties include collection of accounts, care of a stock or merchandise and similar activities and where an audit is necessary or customary in order to determine the net amount due. ­­-------- (RSMo 1939 § 5082, A.L. 1943 p. 410 § 76, A.L. 1963 p. 414, A.L. 1972 H.B. 1203) Prior revisions: 1929 § 4610; 1919 § 9804 ---- end of effective 28 Aug 1972 ---- use this link to bookmark section 290.110 Click here for the Reorganization Act of 1974 - or - Concurrent Resolutions Having Force & Effect of Law In accordance with Section 3.090 , the language of statutory sections enacted durin
perturbation.justification: The changed text contradicts Missouri law regarding prompt payment of wages. Snippet 1 (RSMo Section 290.110) states that when an employee is discharged, 'the unpaid wages of the servant or employee then earned at the contract rate...shall be and become due and payable on the day of the discharge or refusal to longer employ'. The modified contract extends the payment period to 'within 90 days of the Remarketing Settlement Date', directly conflicting with the law's requirement for immediate payment. The change from wire transfer to 'company check' also introduces a potential delay, further undermining the prompt payment requirement, since a check has to be processed. This contradiction is significant as it violates the legally mandated timeframe for wage payment.

perturbation.original_text: Section 16. Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the laws of the State of New York.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 16. Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the laws of the State of California.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law to California introduces potential inconsistencies as certain aspects of the contract (particularly those concerning employment or securities) may be interpreted differently under California law compared to New York law, or Missouri law (where the company is incorporated).
perturbation.law_explanation: California Corporations Code Section 2115 states that certain provisions of California law may apply to foreign corporations (like Reinsurance Group of America, incorporated in Missouri) if specific conditions related to their business activities and shareholder distribution are met. Therefore, stipulating only California law governs the entire agreement can create confusion and contradiction, particularly if Missouri law also applies due to the company's incorporation and principal place of business.

CORPORATIONS [100 - 14707] ( Title 1 enacted by Stats. 1947, Ch. 1038. ) DIVISION 1. GENERAL CORPORATION LAW [100 - 2319] ( Division 1 repealed and added by Stats. 1975, Ch. 682. ) CHAPTER 21. Foreign Corporations [2100 - 2117.1] ( Chapter 21 added by Stats. 1975, Ch. 682. ) 2115. (a) A foreign corporation (other than a foreign association or foreign nonprofit corporation but including a foreign parent corporation even though it does not itself transact intrastate business) is subject to the requirements of subdivision (b) commencing on the date specified in subdivision (d) and continuing until the date specified in subdivision (e) if: (1) The average of the property factor, the payroll factor, and the sales factor (as defined in Sections 25129, 25132, and 25134 of the Revenue and Taxation Code) with respect to it is more than 50 percent during its latest full income year and (2) more than one-half of its outstanding voting securities are held of record by persons having addresses in this state appearing on the books of the corporation on the record date for the latest meeting of shareholders held during its latest full income year or, if no meeting was held during that year, on the last day of the latest full income year. The prope
perturbation.justification: The change from New York to California governing law introduces a contradiction because California Corporations Code Section 2115, as indicated in Scraped Law Snippet 1, might apply to foreign corporations operating in California. Reinsurance Group of America is incorporated in Missouri. The Law Explanation specifies that stipulating only California law may create confusion if Missouri law also applies. Therefore, the changed text directly contradicts the potential applicability of Missouri law, as it unilaterally designates California law. This creates an inconsistency by not accounting for the possible overlay of Missouri law based on the company's incorporation.

perturbation.original_text: Section 11. Termination. This Agreement shall terminate (i) the Business Day immediately following the Remarketing Settlement Date, (ii) at 5:00 p.m., New York City time, on the last date of the Remarketing if the Remarketing is not successful or (iii) on the effective date of the resignation or removal of the Remarketing Agent and the appointment of a new Remarketing Agent.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 11. Termination. This Agreement shall terminate (i) 18 months following the Remarketing Settlement Date, (ii) at 5:00 p.m., New York City time, on the last date of the Remarketing if the Remarketing is not successful or (iii) on the effective date of the resignation or removal of the Remarketing Agent and the appointment of a new Remarketing Agent.
perturbation.explanation: Extending the minimum term of the agreement to 18 months may constitute an unreasonable restraint on trade or service, depending on the specific details of the remarketing activities. This violates general principles of contract law regarding reasonable contract durations.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 1 of the Sherman Antitrust Act prohibits contracts that unreasonably restrain trade. While this act primarily targets agreements between competitors, excessively long-term contracts for services (like this remarketing agreement) can be challenged if they significantly impede competition or access to services in the relevant market. An automatic 18-month term may be viewed as an unreasonable restraint, depending on the industry context and justifications for the term length.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 15 U.S. Code § 1 - Trusts, etc., in restraint of trade illegal; penalty | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 15 U.S. Code § 1 - Trusts, etc., in restraint of trade illegal; penalty U.S. Code Notes prev | next Every contract, combination in the form of trust or otherwise, or conspiracy, in restraint of trade or commerce among the several States, or with foreign nations, is declared to be illegal. Every person who shall make any contract or engage in any combination or conspiracy hereby declared to be illegal shall be deemed guilty of a felony, and, on conviction thereof, shall be punished by fine not exceeding $100,000,000 if a corporation, or, if any other person, $1,000,000, or by imprisonment not exceeding 10 years, or by both said punishments, in the discretion of the court. (July 2, 1890, ch. 647, § 1, 26 Stat. 209 ; Aug. 17, 1937, ch. 690, title VIII, 50 Stat. 693 ; July 7, 1955, ch. 281, 69 Stat. 282 ; Pub. L. 93–528, § 3 , Dec. 21, 1974 , 88 Stat. 1708 ; Pub. L. 94–145, § 2 , Dec. 12, 1975 , 89 Stat. 801 ; Pub. L. 101–588, § 4(a) , Nov. 16, 1990 , 104 Stat. 2880 ; Pub. L. 108–237, title II, § 215(a) , June 22, 2004 , 118 Stat. 668 .) perturbation.justification: The modified text extends the agreement's minimum term to 18 months, which could be seen as an unreasonable restraint of trade under Section 1 of the Sherman Antitrust Act, as it potentially impedes competition. The scraped law snippet states, 'Every contract, combination in the form of trust or otherwise, or conspiracy, in restraint of trade or commerce among the several States, or with foreign nations, is declared to be illegal.' The automatic 18-month term may be an unreasonable restraint, depending on the industry and the lack of justification for the extended duration, thus creating a contradiction.
